.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:305:          lineage.generation: 1
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:306:          lineage.continuedFromRun: null
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:330:        content: '{"openQuestions":[],"resolvedQuestions":[],"keyFindings":[],"ruledOutDirections":[],"metrics":{"iterationsCompleted":0,"openQuestions":0,"resolvedQuestions":0,"keyFindings":0,"convergenceScore":0,"coverageBySources":{}}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:342:        note: "When resource_map_present is true, append the resource_map_known_context snapshot. When false, append 'resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate'."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:406:        note: "This enforces the authored-spec markdown structural contract without applying full-packet required-file or completeness checks to workflow-owned research artifacts. It uses the Node validation orchestrator, whose strict path was measured at about 108ms for a fresh Level 3 packet on the local harness."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:441:          - resource_map_prompt_line: "If resource_map_present is true, return 'Resource map: prior-inventoried files are listed in {spec_folder}/resource-map.md; treat them as the exclusion set when hunting for net-new files; flag only missed-from-map candidates as gaps.' Else return 'Resource map: resource-map.md not present; skipping coverage gate.'"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:450:          log_event: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"userPaused","sentinelPath":"{state_paths.pause_sentinel}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:457:        action: "Evaluate coverage-graph convergence before the inline 3-signal vote and persist the graph decision snapshot"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:458:        bash: 'node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/convergence.cjs --spec-folder "{spec_folder}" --loop-type "research" --session-id "{config.lineage.sessionId}"'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:467:        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"decision":{graph_decision_json},"signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:487:                  coverage = answered / total questions
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:488:                  stop if coverage >= 0.85
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:500:                 gate_key_question_coverage_pass = no blocker named questionCoverage

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:961:      step_fanout_merge:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:962:        description: "Merge N lineage registries into one consolidated registry; skip when single-executor"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:963:        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:965:          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:967:            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:998:        action: "Emit {state_paths.resource_map_output} from converged research deltas unless config.resource_map.emit is false"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1024:          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred — see `deep-research/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branch Contract`.)
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1070:      step_stage_artifact_dir:
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1071:        action: "Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1072:        command: "git add {state_paths.artifact_dir}"
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1073:        note: "Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add research-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal — log warning and continue."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml:1074:        on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:3:description: "New deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs implementing runCappedPool (generalize council multi-seat-dispatch with a concurrency cap) + orchestration-status.log JSONL + orchestration-summary.json."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:6:  - "fanout-pool.cjs capped pool"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:11:    packet_pointer: "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:14:    recent_action: "Phase 2 complete — fanout-pool.cjs (runCappedPool, settleItem, buildPoolSummary, appendStatusLedger, writeOrchestrationSummary) + 10 unit tests green; 171/171 full suite"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:27:Provide the concurrency-capped fan-out orchestrator primitive (the one thing the council dispatcher lacks).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:30:- New `deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs` (script-entry contract like `scripts/convergence.cjs`: tsx bootstrap, JSON-out, exit 0/1/2/3 via `scripts/lib/cli-guards.cjs`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:31:- `runCappedPool({items, concurrency, worker})`: ≤concurrency promises in flight; per-item try/catch (one failure never sinks the pool); allSettled-style results with timing/status (mirror `multi-seat-dispatch.cjs`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:32:- Status ledger: `{artifact_dir}/orchestration-status.log` (JSONL per-lineage events) + `{artifact_dir}/orchestration-summary.json` (`total/succeeded/failed/salvaged/perLineage[]`).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:35:- New `tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts` (modeled on `tests/council/multi-seat-dispatch.vitest.ts`): cap respected; isolation on reject; all-fail summary; ledger shape. Full vitest green.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:38:Per-lineage spawn (003); the `worker` is mockable here.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/graph-metadata.json:196:    "causal_summary": "Add an **opt-in fan-out layer ABOVE the existing single-executor sequential loop** for both `/deep:start-research-loop` and `/deep:start-review-loop` (which share the `deep-loop-runtime` peer skill). N executor \"lineages\" run concurrently with a concurrency cap; each lineage runs the **existing loop verbatim** in its own isolated sub-packet; a consumer-specific cross-lineage merge produces the canonical output (`research.md` / `review-report.md`).",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/graph-metadata.json:199:    "save_lineage": "same_pass",

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:3:description: "New scripts/fanout-pool.cjs implementing runCappedPool (concurrency-capped generalization of council multi-seat-dispatch with never-throws per-item settlement + ordered results) plus settleItem, buildPoolSummary, appendStatusLedger, writeOrchestrationSummary. 10/10 new unit tests pass."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:6:  - "fanout-pool capped pool done"

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:14:    recent_action: "Phase 002 complete: fanout-pool.cjs + 10 tests; full suite 171/171 green"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:15:    next_safe_action: "Phase 003: spawn worker + --artifact-dir-override YAML branch"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:28:1. **`.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-pool.cjs`** (NEW):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:29:   - `runCappedPool({ items, concurrency, worker, now?, onEvent? })` — runs items through an async `worker` with at most `concurrency` in flight (clamped >=1). Generalizes council `dispatchCouncilSeats` by adding the concurrency cap (the `xargs -P K` shape) while keeping its never-throws settlement + ordered results. Returns `{ results: [...ordered], summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }`. Empty pool => `all_failed:false`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:34:   - The `worker` is INJECTED (pure primitive); the real per-lineage spawn worker + the CLI entry (arg parse / JSON-out / exit codes via `cli-guards.cjs`) are Phase 003.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:36:2. **`.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts`** (NEW, 10 tests):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:37:   - `runCappedPool`: cap-not-exceeded (deterministic gated worker via macrotask flush + entered/in-flight tracking), input-order preserved under out-of-order completion, single-failure isolation, all-failed summary, empty pool, concurrency<1 clamps to 1, per-item ledger events (started+completed / started+failed), invalid-args TypeErrors.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/implementation-summary.md:44:- `fanout-pool.vitest.ts`: 10/10 pass.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:2:  "specFolder": "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:3:  "description": "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator can request a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) through /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and the shared deep-loop-runtime. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over the existing fan-out machinery via a thin iterationPlan flag that desugars one-to-one into ordered lineages plus a minimal sequencing hint reusing step_fanout_spawn, fanout-pool.cjs, fanout-run.cjs, fanout-salvage.cjs, fanout-merge.cjs, and executor-config.ts. Produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:10:    "single-executor lineages",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:11:    "fanout-pool",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:12:    "fanout-merge",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:13:    "executor-config",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:14:    "band priority merge precedence",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule/description.json:25:    "123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:3:description: "Full build history for packet 123 — opt-in N-executor fan-out for /deep:start-research-loop and /deep:start-review-loop, generalizing the manual multi-model pattern proven in packet 122 into a first-class command-driven feature."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:6:  - "deep-loop fanout changelog"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:7:  - "parallel fanout changelog"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:19:> Spec folder: `skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:20:> Sessions: §4.A design resolution → Phase 3 spawn + isolation → Phase 4 salvage → Phase 5 merges → Phase 6 docs + parity
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:24:The §4.A architectural blocker was resolved with a new design: **every executor kind uses its own native dispatch mechanism** for fan-out. CLI executor kinds (codex, claude-code, opencode, gemini, devin) run N headless subprocesses via a worker pool; `native` runs N sequential `@deep-research`/`@deep-review` YAML agent dispatches. Mixed configs are supported. This resolved the core problem that the loop has no headless binary — each CLI subprocess is told to run the full loop autonomously in its own isolated sub-packet.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:26:Phases 3–6 were then built straight through: the spawn/isolation layer, a write-failure salvage mechanism, cross-lineage merges with strongest-restriction review rollup, and the full command surface. Single-executor mode is byte-identical to pre-change `main` — verified structurally via `skip_when` guards and the `if_absent` resolver branch.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:34:**Phase 3 — Per-lineage spawn + sub-packet isolation**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:36:- **`scripts/fanout-run.cjs`** — CLI lineage pool driver. TSX-bootstrapped; uses `parseFanoutConfig` / `expandLineages` from `executor-config.ts` and `runCappedPool` from `fanout-pool.cjs`. Filters to CLI lineages only (`kind !== 'native'`); native lineages are dispatched by the YAML. For each CLI lineage: creates `{base}/lineages/{label}/` + `{label}/.executor-state/` dirs; builds a "run the full loop" prompt; constructs the per-kind CLI command (`codex exec`, `claude -p`, `opencode run`, `gemini`, `devin --print`); sets `SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID={label}` + `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` per-lineage to prevent same-kind replica lockfile collisions; runs via `spawnSync` with a generous timeout derived from `lineage.iterations * lineage.timeoutSeconds * 2` (capped at 4 hours); saves stdout to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`; calls `runSalvageSweep` after each subprocess exits. Exit codes: 0=all ok, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:38:- **`003/decision-record.md`** — §4.A locked with an ADR documenting the executor-class dispatch strategy, constraints, alternatives considered, and consequences. Alternatives: Option A (native = single only) rejected because user requires native fan-out; Option B-JS-loop (re-implement loop control in JS) rejected in favour of invoking the full loop command per lineage; Option C (orchestrating agent dispatches all) rejected for CLI kinds.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:40:- **`step_resolve_artifact_root` updated in all 4 YAMLs** — Added `branch_on: "config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir"`. When the override is present (a lineage subprocess received it), `artifact_dir` is bound directly — no `resolveArtifactRoot` node call. When absent (single-executor mode), the `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original command. Affects `deep_start-{research,review}-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:42:- **`step_fanout_spawn` added to all 4 YAMLs** — New top-level workflow step with `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. When config.fanout is present: spawns CLI lineages via `fanout-run.cjs` (`step_fanout_spawn_cli`); dispatches native lineages as sequential `agent: deep-research`/`deep-review` runs (`step_fanout_spawn_native`); after all lineages converge, jumps to `phase_synthesis` skipping `phase_init` and `phase_main_loop`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:44:- **`tests/unit/fanout-run.vitest.ts`** — 5 integration tests: native-only config returns early with ok (no pool spawn); bad JSON config exits 3; missing required args exits 3; 2 cli-codex lineages create distinct `lineages/{label}/` dirs + `orchestration-summary.json` + `orchestration-status.log`; same-kind replicas get distinct `.executor-state` dirs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:46:**Phase 4 — Salvage sweep + coverage-graph per-sessionId**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:48:- **`scripts/fanout-salvage.cjs`** — Pure CJS module (no tsx bootstrap needed, fully testable via `require`). Exports `extractTextFromOpencodeJson` and `runSalvageSweep`. `extractTextFromOpencodeJson` parses opencode `--format json` JSONL lines (`{type:"text",part:{text:"..."}}`) and concatenates text parts; falls back to raw stdout if no JSON parts found; returns null for content under 50 chars. `runSalvageSweep` reads the lineage state log to discover which iterations ran, checks each iteration `.md` file, recovers missing/empty ones from the saved stdout (writing the recovered text and appending a `salvaged_from_stdout` JSONL event), or writes a `fanout_salvage_failed` placeholder when unrecoverable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:50:- **`fanout-run.cjs` worker updated** — After `spawnSync` returns: saves `result.stdout` to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`; calls `runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout)`; includes salvage stats in the returned worker result.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:52:- **`tests/unit/fanout-salvage.vitest.ts`** — 11 tests: `extractTextFromOpencodeJson` (5: null input, opencode JSON parts, raw fallback, too-short fallback, non-text JSON skipped); `runSalvageSweep` unit (5: no state log, all files present, salvage from opencode stdout, failed marker when no content, mixed present/missing); coverage-graph per-sessionId isolation integration (1: two lineages with distinct session_ids insert nodes → each session sees only its own nodes, shared SQLite, no schema change).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:54:**Phase 5 — Consumer merges + synthesis hooks**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:56:- **`scripts/fanout-merge.cjs`** — Cross-lineage merge script. Reads every `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/` sub-packet registry + state log; produces a consolidated registry at the base artifact dir. Research merge: deduplicates `keyFindings` by `id` field, keeping the first-seen entry and appending a `_lineages` attribution array; aggregates total iteration count and averages convergence score; merges `openQuestions` and `ruledOutDirections` similarly. Review merge: applies **strongest-restriction** — iterates active findings across all lineages, escalates to highest severity for duplicate `findingId`, counts active P0/P1/P2, derives `mergedVerdict` (FAIL if activeP0 > 0, CONDITIONAL if activeP1 > 0, PASS otherwise). Both write `{artifact_dir}/fanout-attribution.md` with a per-lineage table (label, kind, model, iterations, convergence score, salvaged count, verdict). Main exits 0=ok, 3=input validation. Exports `mergeResearchRegistries`, `mergeReviewRegistries`, `buildAttributionMd` for unit testing.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:58:- **`step_fanout_merge` added to top of `phase_synthesis` in all 4 YAMLs** — `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"`. Calls `fanout-merge.cjs` with the base `artifact_dir`. Review variant adds `bind_from_output: {p0_count: "active_p0", p1_count: "active_p1", p2_count: "active_p2"}` so `step_derive_verdict` uses merged counts rather than an empty single-executor state log. Research variant feeds the merged `deep-research-findings-registry.json` into the existing `step_compile_research` unchanged.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:60:- **`tests/unit/fanout-merge.vitest.ts`** — 10 tests: `mergeResearchRegistries` (3: dedup by id + cross-lineage tracking, total iter aggregation, null registry handled); `mergeReviewRegistries` strongest-restriction (5: clean+P0→FAIL, all clean→PASS, P1-only→CONDITIONAL, duplicate findingId escalates severity, non-active findings ignored); `fanout-merge.cjs` script e2e (2: no lineages dir → ok, 2-lineage review fixture → merged FAIL registry + attribution.md).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:64:- **`commands/deep/start-research-loop.md`** — argument-hint updated with fan-out flags; Default Resolution Table gains `fanout_executors` and `fanout_concurrency` rows; PARSE step 3 gains `--executor` (repeatable, with `--model/--reasoning-effort/--service-tier/--executor-timeout/--iters/--label/--count` per group), `--executors <json>` escape hatch, `--concurrency N`, and the default policy (0–1 executor → `config.executor`; 2+ / `--executors` / `count>1` → `config.fanout`); EXAMPLES updated with 3 fan-out scenarios (2-CLI parallel, native+CLI mixed, JSON escape hatch) and a native fan-out note.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:68:- **`deep-loop-runtime/SKILL.md`** — Script table extended with 4 new rows (`fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, `fanout-merge.cjs`). Fan-out isolation invariant documented: per-lineage `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` override, per-lineage `session_id` for coverage-graph DB writes (no schema change), per-kind `SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR` for lockfile isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:70:- **`deep-research/SKILL.md`** — NEVER #9 carve-out: command-driven fan-out via `step_fanout_spawn` is SUPPORTED; ad-hoc shell fan-out and intra-lineage wave remain forbidden. EXPERIMENTAL section updated: multi-lineage fan-out moved from reference-only to live supported (N independent loops, not wave orchestration); intra-lineage wave stays deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:72:- **`deep-review/SKILL.md`** — FORBIDDEN INVOCATION PATTERNS section gains explicit carve-out: command-driven fan-out via `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` is SUPPORTED; intra-lineage wave stays deferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:88:The core architectural question was how to make `native` executor participate in fan-out, given the loop has no headless binary. The chosen approach: each executor kind uses its own native dispatch mechanism.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:89:- CLI kinds: `fanout-run.cjs` spawns N headless CLI subprocesses, each running the full loop end-to-end (reading the skill, following the YAML, converging, writing outputs) with `--artifact-dir-override` isolating its sub-packet. "Native codex sub-agents" = N `codex exec` processes; "native claude sub-agents" = N `claude -p` processes.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:90:- `native`: YAML `step_fanout_spawn_native` dispatches N sequential `@deep-research`/`@deep-review` sub-agents. Not pooled (YAML is sequential), appropriate for count 1–3.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:91:- Mixed configs: CLI pool + native YAML dispatches run as separate sub-steps of `step_fanout_spawn`; results are merged in `phase_synthesis`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:93:This was a deliberate change from the original handover §4.A Option A ("reserve native for single mode only") after user direction that all three executor types should support fan-out.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:97:Review verdict derivation uses strongest-restriction: if ANY lineage reports an active P0 finding, the merged verdict is FAIL regardless of other lineages. P1 without P0 → CONDITIONAL; clean → PASS. The rationale: fan-out is a redundancy mechanism — if one reviewer found a P0, the merged review should surface it, not average it away.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:99:**Single-executor parity guarantee**
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:101:All fan-out steps are gated on `config.fanout` being present:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:102:- `step_resolve_artifact_root`: `if_absent` branch runs the byte-identical original `resolveArtifactRoot` node command when `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir` is not set.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:103:- `step_fanout_spawn`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:104:- `step_fanout_merge`: `skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"` — completely skipped in single mode.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:106:No single-executor code path was modified. The parity gate is structural and was verified by inspection.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:154:| `123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md` | Modified — continuity block completed |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:155:| `123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003–006/spec.md` (×4) | Modified — completion_pct 100 + accurate continuity |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:161:- **Native fan-out parallelism**: `native` lineages dispatch sequentially (YAML is sequential). True parallel native lineages would require the YAML runtime to support concurrent `agent:` dispatches or a background-agent mechanism. Currently appropriate for count 1–3; higher parallelism should use CLI executor kinds.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:162:- **Per-iteration log capture**: `fanout-run.cjs` saves the entire subprocess stdout to `{lineageDir}/logs/fanout-lineage.out`. Salvage operates on this combined output. Per-iteration stdout files (`logs/iter-NNN.out`) as planned in the original handover are not produced by the full-loop subprocess model — the salvage mechanism works at the lineage level instead.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:163:- **Spec-folder validation debt (§4.E)**: parent and children still carry the `FRONTMATTER_MEMORY_BLOCK` warning on session-lineage cross-check. Non-blocking; can be resolved by adding `session_dedup` blocks to the lean children.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:170:> Spec folder: `skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout` (phase parent)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:175:Built the two foundation layers for fan-out without touching any existing behavior. Phase 1 added the multi-executor config schema entirely alongside (not replacing) the existing `executorConfigSchema` / `parseExecutorConfig` / `resolveExecutorConfig`. Phase 2 added the concurrency-capped pool primitive that will drive the actual fan-out once the spawn worker is wired in Phase 3. The §4.A architectural blocker (how to run the loop headlessly per lineage) was identified and documented but not yet resolved — that happens in Session 2.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:185:- **`executor-config.ts` additions** (without modifying existing exports):
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:186:  - `lineageExecutorSchema` = `executorConfigSchema.extend({ label (dir-safe `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/`), count (int≥1, default 1), iterations (int≥1|null, default null) })`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:187:  - `fanoutConfigSchema` = `{ executors: lineageExecutorSchema[] (min 1), concurrency: int≥1 (default 2) }`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:191:- **`executor-audit.ts` additions**:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:192:  - Optional `lineageId?: string` on `RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` (type ~L95-106).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:193:  - `buildExecutorAuditRecord(executor, lineageId?)` (~L490) spreads `lineageId` CONDITIONALLY — records are byte-identical when `lineageId` is absent.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:195:- **`executor-config.vitest.ts`** — 9 new fan-out tests: `parseFanoutConfig` happy path, unique-label enforcement, expanded-label collision detection, per-entry kind validation reuse (`cli-codex` requires model), `expandLineages` count expansion. 36 tests total (27 original + 9 new).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:199:- **`scripts/fanout-pool.cjs`** — Pure CJS module (no tsx). Exports:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:200:  - `runCappedPool({ items, concurrency, worker, now?, onEvent? })` — ≤concurrency in flight (clamped ≥1), never-throws per-item settlement, ordered results, returns `{ results, summary: {total, succeeded, failed, all_failed} }`. Worker is injected — the real per-lineage worker is Phase 3.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:206:- **`tests/unit/fanout-pool.vitest.ts`** — 10 tests: concurrency cap (max N in flight at once), single failing item isolated (pool continues), all-fail summary, ordered results, onEvent ledger emission, empty pool, gated-worker timing assertions.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:214:| `buildExecutorAuditRecord` without lineageId — byte-identical to pre-change | PASS |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/changelog.md:215:| `runCappedPool` respects concurrency cap under gated workers | PASS |
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs:111: *   (merged with DEFAULT_WEIGHTS).
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs:149: * Compute pairwise verdict deltas across a sequence of adjudicator
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs:152: * Stability is declared when the last two consecutive pairwise deltas
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs:159: * @returns {Object} Structured progression result with deltas,
.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/council/adjudicator-verdict-scoring.cjs:164:    return { deltas: [], consecutive_stable_rounds: 0, stable: false };

     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     4	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out CLI Lineage Pool Runner                      ║
     5	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     6	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--spec-folder, --loop-type, --fanout-config-json,      ║
     7	// ║         --base-artifact-dir).                                            ║
     8	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     9	// ║ Exit:   0=all ok, 1=script error, 2=some failed, 3=all failed.          ║
    10	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    11	
    12	'use strict';
    13	
    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	// 1. IMPORTS
    16	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17	
    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const os = require('node:os');
    20	const path = require('node:path');
    21	const { spawnSync } = require('node:child_process');
    22	
    23	const {
    24	  classifyExitCode,
    25	  installSignalHandlers,
    26	  maybeThrowTestFault,
    27	  validateNamespaceValue,
    28	} = require('./lib/cli-guards.cjs');
    29	
    30	const {
    31	  runCappedPool,
    32	  appendStatusLedger,
    33	  writeOrchestrationSummary,
    34	} = require('./fanout-pool.cjs');
    35	
    36	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    37	// 2. TSX BOOTSTRAP
    38	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    39	
    40	const TSX_LOADER = path.resolve(
    41	  __dirname,
    42	  '..',
    43	  '..',
    44	  'system-spec-kit',
    45	  'scripts',
    46	  'node_modules',
    47	  'tsx',
    48	  'dist',
    49	  'loader.mjs',
    50	);
    51	
    52	if (process.env.DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED !== '1') {
    53	  const child = spawnSync(
    54	    process.execPath,
    55	    ['--import', TSX_LOADER, __filename, ...process.argv.slice(2)],
    56	    {
    57	      cwd: process.cwd(),
    58	      env: { ...process.env, DEEP_LOOP_TSX_LOADED: '1' },
    59	      input: process.stdin.isTTY ? undefined : fs.readFileSync(0),
    60	      encoding: 'utf8',
    61	    },
    62	  );
    63	  if (child.stdout) process.stdout.write(child.stdout);
    64	  if (child.stderr) process.stderr.write(child.stderr);
    65	  process.exit(child.status === null ? 1 : child.status);
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    69	// 3. HELPERS
    70	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    71	
    72	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    73	  const args = {};
    74	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    75	    const token = argv[i];
    76	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    77	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    78	    }
    79	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    80	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    81	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    82	      args[key] = true;
    83	    } else {
    84	      args[key] = next;
    85	      i += 1;
    86	    }
    87	  }
    88	  return args;
    89	}
    90	
    91	function inputError(message) {
    92	  const err = new Error(message);
    93	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    94	  return err;
    95	}
    96	
    97	function ensureString(args, key) {
    98	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    99	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
   100	  }
   101	  return args[key];
   102	}
   103	
   104	function jsonOut(payload) {
   105	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
   106	}
   107	
   108	// Per-kind first state-dir env var (used for lockfile isolation across same-kind replicas).
   109	const SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND = {
   110	  'cli-codex': 'SPECKIT_CODEX_STATE_DIR',
   111	  'cli-gemini': 'SPECKIT_GEMINI_STATE_DIR',
   112	  'cli-claude-code': 'SPECKIT_CLAUDE_CODE_STATE_DIR',
   113	  'cli-opencode': 'SPECKIT_OPENCODE_STATE_DIR',
   114	  'cli-devin': 'SPECKIT_DEVIN_STATE_DIR',
   115	};
   116	
   117	/**
   118	 * Build the "run the full loop" prompt text for a CLI lineage subprocess.
   119	 * The subprocess reads this, loads the skill, and runs the loop to convergence
   120	 * with config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir overriding step_resolve_artifact_root.
   121	 */
   122	function buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage) {
   123	  const skillFile =
   124	    loopType === 'review'
   125	      ? '.opencode/skills/deep-review/SKILL.md'
   126	      : '.opencode/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md';
   127	  const agentName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review' : 'deep-research';
   128	  return [
   129	    `You are a ${agentName} agent running a fan-out lineage.`,
   130	    ``,
   131	    `Read ${skillFile} and execute the ${loopType} loop with these parameters:`,
   132	    `  spec_folder: ${specFolder}`,
   133	    `  config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: ${lineageDir}`,
   134	    `  session_id: ${sessionId}`,
   135	    `  executor: ${lineage.kind} model=${lineage.model || 'default'}`,
   136	    `  loop_type: ${loopType}`,
   137	    ``,
   138	    `The step_resolve_artifact_root step will bind artifact_dir to ${lineageDir} via the`,
   139	    `config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir override — do NOT run the resolveArtifactRoot node`,
   140	    `command; bind artifact_dir directly to the override value.`,
   141	    ``,
   142	    `Run phase_init, phase_main_loop (to convergence), and phase_synthesis.`,
   143	    `Write all outputs to ${lineageDir}. Do not touch any path outside ${lineageDir}.`,
   144	    ``,
   145	    `When complete, output a single line: FANOUT_LINEAGE_COMPLETE:${lineage.label}`,
   146	  ].join('\n');
   147	}
   148	
   149	/**
   150	 * Compute a generous timeout for a full lineage loop run.
   151	 * A single iteration is timeoutSeconds; a full loop is up to iterations * timeoutSeconds.
   152	 * We double it for safety and cap at 4 hours.
   153	 */
   154	function computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage) {
   155	  const iters = lineage.iterations || 12;
   156	  const perIterSecs = lineage.timeoutSeconds || 900;
   157	  return Math.min(iters * perIterSecs * 2 * 1000, 4 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
   158	}
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Build the subprocess command for a CLI lineage.

   192	    };
   193	  }

   242	    };
   243	  }
   244	
   245	  throw inputError(`Unknown CLI executor kind: ${kind}`);
   246	}

     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Worker Pool                                   ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Concurrency-capped fan-out primitive for the opt-in multi-executor        ║
     5	// ║ ("fan-out") layer above the single-executor deep-loop. Generalizes the    ║
     6	// ║ council parallel dispatcher (lib/council/multi-seat-dispatch.cjs) by      ║
     7	// ║ adding a concurrency cap so N executor lineages run with at most K in      ║
     8	// ║ flight, plus a status-ledger writer mirroring the proven packet-122       ║
     9	// ║ orchestration-status.log.                                                  ║
    10	// ║                                                                           ║
    11	// ║ Design: pure pool primitive (worker is INJECTED) + ledger helpers, fully  ║
    12	// ║ unit-tested. The real spawn worker and the CLI entry that wires it        ║
    13	// ║ (arg parse, JSON-out, exit codes) live in fanout-run.cjs.                ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    19	// 1. IMPORTS
    20	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21	
    22	const fs = require('node:fs');
    23	const path = require('node:path');
    24	
    25	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    26	// 2. HELPERS
    27	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    28	
    29	function isRecord(value) {
    30	  return value !== null && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value);
    31	}
    32	
    33	function normalizeTimestamp(value) {
    34	  if (value instanceof Date) {
    35	    return value.toISOString();
    36	  }
    37	  if (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim() !== '') {
    38	    return value;
    39	  }
    40	  return new Date().toISOString();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function normalizeConcurrency(value) {
    44	  const n = Number(value);
    45	  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n < 1) {
    46	    return 1;
    47	  }
    48	  return Math.floor(n);
    49	}
    50	
    51	function labelFor(item, index) {
    52	  if (isRecord(item) && typeof item.label === 'string' && item.label.trim() !== '') {
    53	    return item.label;
    54	  }
    55	  return `item-${index}`;
    56	}
    57	
    58	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    60	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Run a single pool item through the worker and capture its result or error.
    64	 *
    65	 * Never throws: a worker rejection is captured as a `rejected` result so one
    66	 * failing lineage cannot sink the whole pool (mirrors council `settleSeat`).
    67	 *
    68	 * @param {Object} params - Settlement parameters.
    69	 * @param {*} params.item - The work item (e.g. a lineage descriptor).
    70	 * @param {number} params.index - Zero-based item index (preserves order).
    71	 * @param {Function} params.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
    72	 * @param {Function} params.now - Callable returning a Date or ISO timestamp.
    73	 * @param {Function} [params.onEvent] - Optional callback for ledger events.
    74	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} Result with label, status, timing, output|error.
    75	 */
    76	async function settleItem({ item, index, worker, now, onEvent }) {
    77	  const label = labelFor(item, index);
    78	  const startedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    79	  const startedAtMs = Date.now();
    80	
    81	  if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    82	    onEvent({ event: 'started', label, index, at: startedAtIso });
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  try {
    86	    const output = await worker(item, { index });
    87	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
    88	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
    89	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
    90	      onEvent({ event: 'completed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs });
    91	    }
    92	    return {
    93	      label,
    94	      status: 'fulfilled',
    95	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
    96	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
    97	      duration_ms: durationMs,
    98	      output,
    99	    };
   100	  } catch (error) {
   101	    const completedAtIso = normalizeTimestamp(now());
   102	    const durationMs = Math.max(0, Date.now() - startedAtMs);
   103	    const normalizedError = {
   104	      name: error && error.name ? String(error.name) : 'Error',
   105	      message: error && error.message ? String(error.message) : String(error),
   106	    };
   107	    if (typeof onEvent === 'function') {
   108	      onEvent({ event: 'failed', label, index, at: completedAtIso, duration_ms: durationMs, error: normalizedError });
   109	    }
   110	    return {
   111	      label,
   112	      status: 'rejected',
   113	      started_at_iso: startedAtIso,
   114	      completed_at_iso: completedAtIso,
   115	      duration_ms: durationMs,
   116	      error: normalizedError,
   117	    };
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Run items through a worker with at most `concurrency` in flight.
   123	 *
   124	 * This is the fan-out generalization of council `dispatchCouncilSeats`: same
   125	 * never-throws per-item settlement and ordered results, plus a concurrency cap
   126	 * so a large lineage set runs K-at-a-time (the proven `xargs -P K` shape).
   127	 *
   128	 * @param {Object} options - Pool configuration.
   129	 * @param {Array} options.items - Work items (each ideally has a `label`).
   130	 * @param {number} options.concurrency - Max items in flight (clamped to >= 1).
   131	 * @param {Function} options.worker - Async worker: (item, { index }) => output.
   132	 * @param {Function} [options.now] - Callable returning a Date/ISO timestamp.
   133	 * @param {Function} [options.onEvent] - Optional ledger-event callback.
   134	 * @returns {Promise<Object>} { results: [...ordered], summary: { total,
   135	 *   succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   136	 * @throws {TypeError} If items is not an array or worker is not a function.
   137	 */
   138	function runCappedPool(options) {
   139	  if (!isRecord(options)) {
   140	    throw new TypeError('runCappedPool options must be an object');
   141	  }
   142	  const { items, worker } = options;
   143	  if (!Array.isArray(items)) {
   144	    throw new TypeError('items must be an array');
   145	  }
   146	  if (typeof worker !== 'function') {
   147	    throw new TypeError('worker must be a function');
   148	  }
   149	
   150	  const concurrency = normalizeConcurrency(options.concurrency);
   151	  const now = typeof options.now === 'function' ? options.now : () => new Date();
   152	  const onEvent = typeof options.onEvent === 'function' ? options.onEvent : undefined;
   153	  const results = new Array(items.length);
   154	
   155	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   156	    if (items.length === 0) {
   157	      resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   158	      return;
   159	    }
   160	
   161	    let nextIndex = 0;
   162	    let active = 0;
   163	    let resolved = false;
   164	
   165	    const pump = () => {
   166	      if (resolved) {
   167	        return;
   168	      }
   169	      if (nextIndex >= items.length && active === 0) {
   170	        resolved = true;
   171	        resolve(buildPoolSummary(results));
   172	        return;
   173	      }
   174	      while (active < concurrency && nextIndex < items.length) {
   175	        const index = nextIndex;
   176	        nextIndex += 1;
   177	        active += 1;
   178	        settleItem({ item: items[index], index, worker, now, onEvent })
   179	          .then((result) => {
   180	            results[index] = result;
   181	          })
   182	          .catch((error) => {
   183	            // settleItem never throws, but stay defensive so the pump never wedges.
   184	            results[index] = {
   185	              label: labelFor(items[index], index),
   186	              status: 'rejected',
   187	              error: { name: 'PoolError', message: String(error) },
   188	            };
   189	          })
   190	          .finally(() => {
   191	            active -= 1;
   192	            pump();
   193	          });
   194	      }
   195	    };
   196	
   197	    pump();
   198	  });
   199	}
   200	
   201	/**
   202	 * Build the ordered-results + summary envelope for a completed pool run.
   203	 *
   204	 * @param {Array<Object>} results - Per-item settlement results (ordered).
   205	 * @returns {Object} { results, summary: { total, succeeded, failed, all_failed } }.
   206	 */
   207	function buildPoolSummary(results) {
   208	  const total = results.length;
   209	  const succeeded = results.filter((result) => result && result.status === 'fulfilled').length;
   210	  const failed = total - succeeded;
   211	  return {
   212	    results,
   213	    summary: {
   214	      total,
   215	      succeeded,
   216	      failed,
   217	      all_failed: total > 0 && failed === total,
   218	    },
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   223	// 4. STATUS LEDGER
   224	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Append one JSONL event to the orchestration status ledger.
   228	 *
   229	 * Generalizes the packet-122 `orchestration-status.log`: one line per lineage
   230	 * lifecycle event (started / completed / failed / salvaged / converged).
   231	 *
   232	 * @param {string} ledgerPath - Path to the JSONL ledger file.
   233	 * @param {Object} entry - A serializable event object.
   234	 */
   235	function appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, entry) {
   236	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(ledgerPath), { recursive: true });
   237	  fs.appendFileSync(ledgerPath, `${JSON.stringify(entry)}\n`);
   238	}
   239	
   240	/**
   241	 * Write the orchestration summary JSON for a completed fan-out run.
   242	 *
   243	 * @param {string} summaryPath - Path to the summary JSON file.
   244	 * @param {Object} summary - Summary payload (e.g. the pool summary plus
   245	 *   salvage counts and per-lineage rollups).
   246	 */
   247	function writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, summary) {
   248	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(summaryPath), { recursive: true });
   249	  fs.writeFileSync(summaryPath, `${JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2)}\n`);
   250	}
   251	
   252	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253	// 5. EXPORTS
   254	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   255	
   256	module.exports = {
   257	  runCappedPool,
   258	  settleItem,
   259	  buildPoolSummary,
   260	  appendStatusLedger,

     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Cross-Lineage Merge                          ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Input:  CLI args (--loop-type, --artifact-dir).                          ║
     5	// ║ Output: JSON to stdout.                                                  ║
     6	// ║ Exit:   0=ok, 1=script error, 3=input validation error.                 ║
     7	// ║                                                                          ║
     8	// ║ Reads every {artifact-dir}/lineages/{label}/ sub-packet and produces:   ║
     9	// ║   research: deduplicated deep-research-findings-registry.json +          ║
    10	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    11	// ║   review:   severity-rollup deep-review-findings-registry.json           ║
    12	// ║             (strongest-restriction: any lineage P0 → merged FAIL) +     ║
    13	// ║             fanout-attribution.md                                        ║
    14	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15	
    16	'use strict';
    17	
    18	const fs = require('node:fs');
    19	const path = require('node:path');
    20	
    21	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22	// 1. HELPERS
    23	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    24	
    25	function inputError(message) {
    26	  const err = new Error(message);
    27	  err.code = 'INPUT_VALIDATION';
    28	  return err;
    29	}
    30	
    31	function jsonOut(payload) {
    32	  process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(payload)}\n`);
    33	}
    34	
    35	function parseArgs(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
    36	  const args = {};
    37	  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
    38	    const token = argv[i];
    39	    if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
    40	      throw inputError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
    41	    }
    42	    const key = token.slice(2).replace(/-([a-z])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
    43	    const next = argv[i + 1];
    44	    if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
    45	      args[key] = true;
    46	    } else {
    47	      args[key] = next;
    48	      i += 1;
    49	    }
    50	  }
    51	  return args;
    52	}
    53	
    54	function ensureString(args, key) {
    55	  if (!args[key] || typeof args[key] !== 'string') {
    56	    throw inputError(`${key} is required`);
    57	  }
    58	  return args[key];
    59	}
    60	
    61	function tryReadJson(filePath) {
    62	  if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return null;
    63	  try {
    64	    return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
    65	  } catch {
    66	    return null;
    67	  }
    68	}
    69	
    70	function readStateLog(stateLogPath) {
    71	  if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return [];
    72	  const lines = fs.readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8').trim().split('\n').filter(Boolean);
    73	  return lines.flatMap((line) => {
    74	    try {
    75	      return [JSON.parse(line)];
    76	    } catch {
    77	      return [];
    78	    }
    79	  });
    80	}
    81	
    82	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    83	// 2. RESEARCH MERGE
    84	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    85	
    86	/**
    87	 * Merge research findings registries from all lineages.
    88	 * Deduplicates by findingId; cross-model attribution via lineage labels.
    89	 * Returns the merged registry object.
    90	 */
    91	function mergeResearchRegistries(lineageData) {
    92	  const findingById = new Map();
    93	
    94	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
    95	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.keyFindings)) continue;
    96	    for (const finding of registry.keyFindings) {
    97	      const id = finding.id || finding.title;
    98	      if (!id) continue;
    99	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   100	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   101	        // Add this lineage as a contributing source
   102	        if (!existing._lineages) existing._lineages = [];
   103	        if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   104	      } else {
   105	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   106	      }
   107	    }
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   111	  const openQuestionsById = new Map();
   112	  const ruledOutById = new Map();
   113	
   114	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   115	    if (!registry) continue;
   116	    for (const q of registry.openQuestions ?? []) {
   117	      const id = q.id || q.question;
   118	      if (!id) continue;
   119	      if (!openQuestionsById.has(id)) openQuestionsById.set(id, { ...q, _lineages: [label] });
   120	      else openQuestionsById.get(id)._lineages.push(label);
   121	    }
   122	    for (const d of registry.ruledOutDirections ?? []) {
   123	      const id = d.id || d.direction;
   124	      if (!id) continue;
   125	      if (!ruledOutById.has(id)) ruledOutById.set(id, { ...d, _lineages: [label] });
   126	    }
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  const totalIters = lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => {
   130	    return sum + (registry?.metrics?.iterationsCompleted ?? 0);
   131	  }, 0);
   132	
   133	  const avgConvergence =
   134	    lineageData.length > 0
   135	      ? lineageData.reduce((sum, { registry }) => sum + (registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? 0), 0) /
   136	        lineageData.length
   137	      : 0;
   138	
   139	  return {
   140	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   141	    openQuestions: [...openQuestionsById.values()],
   142	    resolvedQuestions: [],
   143	    keyFindings: mergedFindings,
   144	    ruledOutDirections: [...ruledOutById.values()],
   145	    metrics: {
   146	      iterationsCompleted: totalIters,
   147	      openQuestions: openQuestionsById.size,
   148	      resolvedQuestions: 0,
   149	      keyFindings: mergedFindings.length,
   150	      convergenceScore: Math.round(avgConvergence * 1000) / 1000,
   151	      coverageBySources: {},
   152	    },
   153	  };
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   157	// 3. REVIEW MERGE  (strongest-restriction)
   158	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   159	
   160	const SEVERITY_RANK = { P0: 3, P1: 2, P2: 1 };
   161	
   162	/**
   163	 * Merge review findings registries with strongest-restriction severity rollup.
   164	 * Any lineage with an active P0 finding causes the merged result to be FAIL.
   165	 * Deduplication is by findingId; cross-lineage P0 wins if any lineage reports it.
   166	 */
   167	function mergeReviewRegistries(lineageData) {
   168	  const findingById = new Map();
   169	
   170	  for (const { label, registry } of lineageData) {
   171	    if (!registry || !Array.isArray(registry.openFindings)) continue;
   172	    for (const finding of registry.openFindings) {
   173	      if (finding.status !== 'active') continue;
   174	      const id = finding.findingId || finding.title;
   175	      if (!id) continue;
   176	      if (findingById.has(id)) {
   177	        const existing = findingById.get(id);
   178	        // Strongest-restriction: escalate to highest severity seen across lineages
   179	        if ((SEVERITY_RANK[finding.severity] ?? 0) > (SEVERITY_RANK[existing.severity] ?? 0)) {
   180	          findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [...(existing._lineages || []), label] });
   181	        } else {
   182	          if (!existing._lineages.includes(label)) existing._lineages.push(label);
   183	        }
   184	      } else {
   185	        findingById.set(id, { ...finding, _lineages: [label] });
   186	      }
   187	    }
   188	  }
   189	
   190	  const mergedFindings = [...findingById.values()];
   191	  const activeP0 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P0' && f.status === 'active').length;
   192	  const activeP1 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P1' && f.status === 'active').length;
   193	  const activeP2 = mergedFindings.filter((f) => f.severity === 'P2' && f.status === 'active').length;
   194	
   195	  // Strongest-restriction verdict
   196	  let mergedVerdict;
   197	  if (activeP0 > 0) {
   198	    mergedVerdict = 'FAIL';
   199	  } else if (activeP1 > 0) {
   200	    mergedVerdict = 'CONDITIONAL';
   201	  } else {
   202	    mergedVerdict = 'PASS';
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  return {
   206	    mergedFrom: lineageData.map(({ label }) => label),
   207	    mergedVerdict,
   208	    openFindings: mergedFindings,
   209	    resolvedFindings: [],
   210	    findingsBySeverity: { P0: activeP0, P1: activeP1, P2: activeP2 },
   211	    openFindingsCount: mergedFindings.length,
   212	    resolvedFindingsCount: 0,
   213	    activeP0,
   214	    activeP1,
   215	    activeP2,
   216	  };
   217	}
   218	
   219	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   220	// 4. ATTRIBUTION
   221	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222	
   223	/**
   224	 * Build fanout-attribution.md summarizing per-lineage convergence, iters, salvage, model.
   225	 */
   226	function buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType) {
   227	  const lines = [
   228	    `# Fan-Out Attribution`,
   229	    ``,
   230	    `Loop type: **${loopType}**  |  Lineage count: **${lineageData.length}**`,
   231	    ``,
   232	    `| Label | Kind | Model | Iterations | Convergence | Salvaged | Verdict |`,
   233	    `|-------|------|-------|-----------|-------------|----------|---------|`,
   234	  ];
   235	
   236	  for (const { label, registry, stateRecords, kind, model } of lineageData) {
   237	    const iters = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'iteration').length;
   238	    const salvage = stateRecords.filter((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'salvaged_from_stdout').length;
   239	    const convergenceScore = registry?.metrics?.convergenceScore ?? registry?.convergenceScore ?? 'n/a';
   240	    const verdict =
   241	      loopType === 'review'
   242	        ? registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P0 > 0
   243	          ? 'FAIL'
   244	          : registry?.findingsBySeverity?.P1 > 0
   245	          ? 'CONDITIONAL'
   246	          : 'PASS'
   247	        : 'n/a';
   248	    lines.push(`| ${label} | ${kind ?? 'unknown'} | ${model ?? 'default'} | ${iters} | ${convergenceScore} | ${salvage} | ${verdict} |`);
   249	  }
   250	
   251	  lines.push('');
   252	  return lines.join('\n');
   253	}
   254	
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	// 5. MAIN
   257	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   258	
   259	function main() {
   260	  const args = parseArgs();
   261	  const loopType = ensureString(args, 'loopType');
   262	  if (loopType !== 'research' && loopType !== 'review') {
   263	    throw inputError('loopType must be "research" or "review"');
   264	  }
   265	  const artifactDir = ensureString(args, 'artifactDir');
   266	  const lineagesDir = path.join(artifactDir, 'lineages');
   267	
   268	  if (!fs.existsSync(lineagesDir)) {
   269	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineages directory — nothing to merge', merged: 0 });
   270	    return;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  const labelDirs = fs.readdirSync(lineagesDir).filter((entry) =>
   274	    fs.statSync(path.join(lineagesDir, entry)).isDirectory(),
   275	  );
   276	
   277	  if (labelDirs.length === 0) {
   278	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no lineage subdirs found', merged: 0 });
   279	    return;
   280	  }
   281	
   282	  // Load per-lineage data
   283	  const registryName =
   284	    loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-findings-registry.json' : 'deep-research-findings-registry.json';
   285	  const stateLogName = loopType === 'review' ? 'deep-review-state.jsonl' : 'deep-research-state.jsonl';
   286	  const summaryPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   287	  const orchestrationSummary = tryReadJson(summaryPath) ?? {};
   288	
   289	  const lineageData = labelDirs.map((label) => {
   290	    const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, label);
   291	    const registry = tryReadJson(path.join(lineageDir, registryName));
   292	    const stateRecords = readStateLog(path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName));
   293	    // Infer kind/model from state log executor records
   294	    const executorRecord = stateRecords.find((r) => r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'executor_start');
   295	    return {
   296	      label,
   297	      lineageDir,
   298	      registry,
   299	      stateRecords,
   300	      kind: executorRecord?.kind ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.kind ?? 'unknown',
   301	      model: executorRecord?.model ?? orchestrationSummary?.[label]?.model ?? 'unknown',
   302	    };
   303	  });
   304	
   305	  const lineagesWithRegistry = lineageData.filter((d) => d.registry !== null);
   306	
   307	  let mergedRegistry;
   308	  if (loopType === 'review') {
   309	    mergedRegistry = mergeReviewRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   310	  } else {
   311	    mergedRegistry = mergeResearchRegistries(lineagesWithRegistry);
   312	  }
   313	
   314	  // Write merged registry to base artifact dir (replacing single-executor path)
   315	  const mergedRegistryPath = path.join(artifactDir, registryName);
   316	  fs.writeFileSync(mergedRegistryPath, JSON.stringify(mergedRegistry, null, 2), 'utf8');
   317	
   318	  // Write attribution markdown
   319	  const attributionPath = path.join(artifactDir, 'fanout-attribution.md');
   320	  fs.writeFileSync(attributionPath, buildAttributionMd(lineageData, loopType), 'utf8');
   321	
   322	  jsonOut({
   323	    status: 'ok',
   324	    loop_type: loopType,
   325	    merged_lineages: lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   326	    skipped_no_registry: lineageData.length - lineagesWithRegistry.length,
   327	    merged_registry_path: mergedRegistryPath,
   328	    attribution_path: attributionPath,
   329	    ...(loopType === 'review'
   330	      ? { merged_verdict: mergedRegistry.mergedVerdict, active_p0: mergedRegistry.activeP0, active_p1: mergedRegistry.activeP1 }
   331	      : { key_findings: mergedRegistry.keyFindings?.length ?? 0 }),
   332	  });
   333	}
   334	
   335	// Exports for unit testing
   336	module.exports = { mergeResearchRegistries, mergeReviewRegistries, buildAttributionMd };
   337	
   338	if (require.main === module) {
   339	  try {
   340	    main();
   341	  } catch (err) {
   342	    const code = err && err.code === 'INPUT_VALIDATION' ? 3 : 1;
   343	    jsonOut({
   344	      status: 'error',
   345	      error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
   346	      code: err && err.code ? err.code : 'SCRIPT_ERROR',
   347	    });
   348	    if (code === 1) {
   349	      process.stderr.write(
   350	        JSON.stringify({ error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err), stack: err && err.stack }) + '\n',
   351	      );
   352	    }
   353	    process.exit(code);
   354	  }
   355	}

     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep-Loop Runtime — Fan-Out Lineage Salvage                              ║
     3	// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
     4	// ║ Recovers missing iteration files from captured subprocess stdout when    ║
     5	// ║ a CLI executor fails to write outputs (sandbox write restrictions, etc). ║
     6	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     7	
     8	'use strict';
     9	
    10	const fs = require('node:fs');
    11	const path = require('node:path');
    12	
    13	const STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE = {
    14	  research: 'deep-research-state.jsonl',
    15	  review: 'deep-review-state.jsonl',
    16	};
    17	
    18	/**
    19	 * Parse opencode --format json text parts from subprocess stdout.
    20	 * Opencode emits JSONL lines of {type:"text",part:{text:"..."}}.
    21	 * Concatenates all text parts; falls back to raw stdout for other executors.
    22	 *
    23	 * @param {string|null} stdout - Combined subprocess stdout.
    24	 * @returns {string|null} Recovered text, or null if nothing substantive.
    25	 */
    26	function extractTextFromOpencodeJson(stdout) {
    27	  if (!stdout || typeof stdout !== 'string') return null;
    28	
    29	  const textParts = [];
    30	  for (const line of stdout.split('\n')) {
    31	    const trimmed = line.trim();
    32	    if (!trimmed.startsWith('{')) continue;
    33	    try {
    34	      const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
    35	      if (
    36	        parsed &&
    37	        parsed.type === 'text' &&
    38	        parsed.part &&
    39	        typeof parsed.part.text === 'string'
    40	      ) {
    41	        textParts.push(parsed.part.text);
    42	      }
    43	    } catch {
    44	      // non-JSON line — skip
    45	    }
    46	  }
    47	
    48	  if (textParts.length > 0) return textParts.join('').slice(0, 50_000);
    49	
    50	  const raw = stdout.trim();
    51	  return raw.length > 50 ? raw.slice(0, 50_000) : null;
    52	}
    53	
    54	/**
    55	 * After a lineage subprocess exits, recover any missing or empty iteration
    56	 * files from the saved stdout (write-failure salvage path).
    57	 *
    58	 * For each iteration recorded in the state log that lacks its .md file:
    59	 *   - If recoverable text is available: write it and append a
    60	 *     salvaged_from_stdout event to the state log.
    61	 *   - Otherwise: write a failed-marker placeholder so downstream steps
    62	 *     see a file and can apply their own stuck-recovery logic.
    63	 *
    64	 * @param {string} lineageDir - Absolute path to the lineage artifact dir.
    65	 * @param {'research'|'review'} loopType - Loop type for state log naming.
    66	 * @param {string} savedStdout - Captured subprocess stdout.
    67	 * @returns {{ salvaged: number, failed: number }}
    68	 */
    69	function runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout) {
    70	  const stateLogName = STATE_LOG_BY_LOOP_TYPE[loopType];
    71	  if (!stateLogName) return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
    72	
    73	  const stateLogPath = path.join(lineageDir, stateLogName);
    74	  const iterDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'iterations');
    75	
    76	  if (!fs.existsSync(stateLogPath)) return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
    77	
    78	  // Discover which iterations completed by scanning the state log.
    79	  const iterationNumbers = new Set();
    80	  let stateContent;
    81	  try {
    82	    stateContent = fs.readFileSync(stateLogPath, 'utf8');
    83	  } catch {
    84	    return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
    85	  }
    86	
    87	  for (const line of stateContent.trim().split('\n')) {
    88	    if (!line.trim()) continue;
    89	    try {
    90	      const record = JSON.parse(line);
    91	      if (record && record.type === 'iteration' && typeof record.iteration === 'number') {
    92	        iterationNumbers.add(record.iteration);
    93	      }
    94	    } catch {
    95	      // malformed JSONL — skip
    96	    }
    97	  }
    98	
    99	  if (iterationNumbers.size === 0) return { salvaged: 0, failed: 0 };
   100	
   101	  let salvaged = 0;
   102	  let failed = 0;
   103	  const recoveredText = extractTextFromOpencodeJson(savedStdout);
   104	
   105	  for (const iterNum of iterationNumbers) {
   106	    const iterFile = path.join(iterDir, `iteration-${iterNum}.md`);
   107	
   108	    // Skip iterations that already have a non-empty file.
   109	    if (fs.existsSync(iterFile)) {
   110	      try {
   111	        if (fs.statSync(iterFile).size > 0) continue;
   112	      } catch {
   113	        // stat failed — treat as missing
   114	      }
   115	    }
   116	
   117	    fs.mkdirSync(iterDir, { recursive: true });
   118	
   119	    if (recoveredText) {
   120	      fs.writeFileSync(iterFile, recoveredText, 'utf8');
   121	      const event = JSON.stringify({
   122	        type: 'event',
   123	        event: 'salvaged_from_stdout',
   124	        iteration: iterNum,
   125	        source: 'fanout_lineage_stdout',
   126	        bytes_recovered: recoveredText.length,
   127	      });
   128	      fs.appendFileSync(stateLogPath, event + '\n', 'utf8');
   129	      salvaged += 1;
   130	    } else {
   131	      fs.writeFileSync(
   132	        iterFile,
   133	        `<!-- fanout_salvage_failed: iteration ${iterNum} content not recoverable from subprocess stdout -->\n`,
   134	        'utf8',
   135	      );
   136	      failed += 1;
   137	    }
   138	  }
   139	
   140	  return { salvaged, failed };
   141	}
   142	
   143	module.exports = { runSalvageSweep, extractTextFromOpencodeJson };

codex
One interesting composition point already stands out: the pool’s “fulfilled” state is independent of whether the spawned loop actually produced a registry; the merge layer later skips missing registries. I’m checking whether the runner or command YAML closes that gap before calling it a finding.

   290	  const cliLineages = allLineages.filter((l) => l.kind !== 'native');
   291	
   292	  if (cliLineages.length === 0) {
   293	    jsonOut({ status: 'ok', message: 'no CLI lineages to spawn', results: [], summary: { total: 0, succeeded: 0, failed: 0, all_failed: false } });
   294	    return;
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  const lineagesDir = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'lineages');
   298	  const ledgerPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-status.log');
   299	  const summaryPath = path.join(baseArtifactDir, 'orchestration-summary.json');
   300	  const runId = `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
   301	
   302	  fs.mkdirSync(lineagesDir, { recursive: true });
   303	
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313

   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316

   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });
   349	
   350	      // Save subprocess stdout for salvage sweep (write failures in weak CLI executors).
   351	      const logsDir = path.join(lineageDir, 'logs');
   352	      fs.mkdirSync(logsDir, { recursive: true });
   353	      const savedStdout = typeof result.stdout === 'string' ? result.stdout : '';
   354	      fs.writeFileSync(path.join(logsDir, 'fanout-lineage.out'), savedStdout, 'utf8');
   355	
   356	      // Recover missing iteration files from captured stdout when possible.
   357	      const salvage = runSalvageSweep(lineageDir, loopType, savedStdout);
   358	
   359	      const exitCode = result.status ?? (result.error ? 1 : 0);
   360	      return { label: lineage.label, exitCode, timedOut: result.signal === 'SIGTERM', salvage };
   361	    },
   362	  });
   363	
   364	  writeOrchestrationSummary(summaryPath, {
   365	    run_id: runId,
   366	    loop_type: loopType,
   367	    spec_folder: specFolder,
   368	    base_artifact_dir: baseArtifactDir,
   369	    total_cli_lineages: cliLineages.length,

   371	  });

   375	  process.exit(exitCode);
   376	}
