 succeeded in 0ms:
3:description: "Design (spec only) a first-class contract-compliant per-iteration model schedule for deep-loop fan-out: an operator requests a fixed-count heterogeneous schedule (e.g. nine iterations on minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed then one on claude-opus-4-8) via /deep:start-review-loop, /deep:start-research-loop, and shared deep-loop-runtime, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool like packet 122 review did. Models the schedule as ordered single-executor lineages (bands) over existing fan-out via a thin iterationPlan flag desugaring one-to-one into ordered lineages plus minimal sequencing. Produces schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, command-surface design; implementation is this phase's future work."
11:    packet_pointer: "skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/007-native-per-iteration-model-schedule"
27:Design (spec only) a first-class, contract-compliant way for an operator to request a fixed-count heterogeneous per-iteration model schedule through the canonical `/deep:start-review-loop` and `/deep:start-research-loop` commands and the shared `deep-loop-runtime`. Motivating example: nine iterations on `minimax MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` then one on `claude-opus-4-8`, so future runs never hand-roll a worker pool the way packet 122 review did. This phase produces the schema, routing, audit, merge-precedence, and command-surface design; implementation is this phase's own future work (no code here).
29:**Background — the packet 122 deviation:** packet 122 review ran a heterogeneous per-iteration model mix by hand-rolling a shell worker pool (`run_one.sh` with a `case` statement assigning per-iteration dimensions and a `xargs`-style fan-out). That bespoke machinery bypassed the shared `deep-loop-runtime` dispatch, the externalized JSONL state machine, and the consumer merge; it worked once but is unreproducible through the supported command surface and violates deep-review forbidden patterns.
31:**Problem statement — the contract gap:** there is no supported way to express "run band A on model X for N iterations, then band B on model Y for M iterations" through the canonical commands. Existing fan-out (phases 001-006) runs homogeneous lineages concurrently and merges once all complete; it has no ordered heterogeneous per-iteration schedule and no ordered/seeded band sequencing, forcing operators back toward the packet 122 hand-rolled path.
36:- Schema/flag design: an `iterationPlan` of ordered bands (from-to or count; executor kind and model; optional focus/dimensions; optional dependsOn/order) plus the `iteration-plan` (a.k.a. `model-schedule`) flag desugaring one-to-one into the existing `executors` lineage array, integrated through `step_fanout_spawn`, `step_fanout_spawn_cli`, `fanout-pool.cjs`, `fanout-run.cjs`, `fanout-salvage.cjs`, and `executor-config.ts` (reuse-first; no parallel dispatcher).
39:- Per-iteration/per-band executor audit and tracing (band and model attribution) in the JSONL state log, deltas, and findings registry; plus merge-precedence as an ordered band-priority list in `fanout-merge.cjs` preserving review severity rollup and research dedup/attribution.
46:- Inventing a new parallel orchestration system, worker-pool runtime, or state machine that competes with `step_fanout_spawn` and `fanout-pool.cjs`.
47:- True intra-lineage per-iteration executor rotation inside one continuous loop with cross-lineage iteration interleaving or band-synchronization barriers (rejected in favor of ordered homogeneous lineages unless desugaring proves insufficient).
49:- New executor kinds/providers/models beyond those registered (minimax, codex, claude, opencode, gemini, devin, native) and changes to the single-executor default path or existing homogeneous fan-out merge semantics.
50:- Re-litigating or modifying packet 122 hand-rolled artifacts.
53:- Command surface and one-to-one desugaring into existing fan-out lineages specified; desugaring pinned to real fan-out and `executor-config` entry points.
56:- Merge precedence keeps the severity-rollup invariant (any active P0 from any band forces merged FAIL).
61:- **Sequencing gap:** existing fan-out runs lineages concurrently and jumps to synthesis once all complete; band B after band A with optional seeding needs an ordered/`dependsOn` extension to `fanout-pool.cjs` that does not yet exist, so it may be underestimated as pure sugar. Mitigation: front-load research to confirm whether ordering is sugar over concurrency caps or a genuine minimal pool-driver delta, and scope it explicitly.
64:- **Contract-compliance illusion and two-consumer divergence:** a loosely specified band-priority or ordered layer could resemble the forbidden ad-hoc dispatcher, and research dedup/attribution versus review severity-rollup may fit one design unevenly. Mitigation: explicit per-forbidden-pattern mapping; require all dispatch inside `step_fanout_spawn` and the fan-out scripts; specify the band-priority and audit model for both consumers with differences called out.
68:Implementation/build of the capability; any code edits; a competing parallel orchestrator; true intra-lineage executor rotation; convergence-math changes; new executor kinds; packet 122 artifact changes (all deferred or excluded per Scope/Out above).

  1204	      # H-1 verdict derivation: parse findings JSONL, map highest severity to verdict

  1206	      # - CONDITIONAL: any P1 findings (no P0)
  1207	      # - FAIL: any P0 findings
  1208	      # Final line of iteration-NNN.md MUST be exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" / "Review verdict: CONDITIONAL" / "Review verdict: FAIL"
  1209	      step_derive_verdict:
  1210	        action: "Derive review verdict from accumulated findings"
  1211	        algorithm: |
  1212	          if p0_count > 0:
  1213	            verdict = "FAIL"
  1214	          elif p1_count > 0:
  1215	            verdict = "CONDITIONAL"
  1216	          else:
  1217	            verdict = "PASS"
  1218	          has_advisories = (verdict == "PASS" and p2_count > 0)
  1219	        outputs:
  1220	          - verdict: "PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL"
  1221	          - has_advisories: "Boolean true when verdict is PASS and any active P2 findings exist"
  1222	        contract: |
  1223	          Every iteration-NNN.md must end with exactly: "Review verdict: PASS" (or CONDITIONAL or FAIL).
  1224	          Downstream automation parses this final line via exact string match — do not vary the format.
  1225	
  1226	      step_read_all_iterations:
  1227	        action: "Read all iteration files and strategy"
  1228	        read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
  1229	        read_also: "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1230	
  1231	      step_build_finding_registry:
  1232	        action: "Build deduplicated finding registry from all iterations"
  1233	        algorithm: |
  1234	          1. Extract all findings from iteration JSONL delta files
  1235	          2. Compute content_hash for each finding (emission requirement):
  1236	             content_hash = sha256(file_path + "\u001f" + line_range + "\u001f" + finding_type + "\u001f" + normalized_description_80chars)
  1237	             Normalized description = whitespace-collapsed, lowercased, first 80 chars of the finding description.
  1238	          3. Two-tier dedup:
  1239	             PRIMARY: group by content_hash field
  1240	               - Same content_hash, multiple dimensions → single finding, merged `dimensions: [<all>]` array
  1241	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
  1242	             FALLBACK (legacy records lacking content_hash): file:line + normalized_title
  1243	               - Same file:line, multiple dimensions → single finding, multiple dimension tags
  1244	               - Keep highest severity on duplicates
  1245	          4. Track finding lifecycle:
  1246	             - status: active | duplicate | upgraded | downgraded | contested | resolved_false_positive
  1247	             - Record first_seen, upgraded_in, previous_severity for severity changes
  1248	             - Record dimensions array reflecting all dimensions that emitted this finding
  1249	          5. Resolve contradictions:
  1250	             - Findings marked contested across iterations → referee pass
  1251	             - Determine final severity and status
  1252	          6. Count only 'active' findings for totals
  1253	        outputs:
  1254	          - finding_registry: "Deduplicated list of all findings with metadata"
  1255	          - active_p0: "Count of active P0 findings"
  1256	          - active_p1: "Count of active P1 findings"
  1257	          - active_p2: "Count of active P2 findings"
  1258	
  1259	      step_adversarial_selfcheck:
  1260	        action: "Final adversarial self-check on all P0 and P1 findings"
  1261	        algorithm: |
  1262	          For each active P0/P1 finding:
  1263	            1. Hunter: Re-verify finding against source code (re-read file:line)
  1264	            2. Skeptic: Challenge severity — is this really P0/P1? Could it be P2?
  1265	            3. Referee: Final call — confirm, downgrade, or mark false_positive
  1266	          Update finding_registry with adjudicated results.
  1267	
  1268	      step_emit_resource_map:
  1269	        action: "Emit {state_paths.resource_map_output} from converged review deltas unless config.resource_map.emit is false"
  1270	        command: "node .opencode/skills/deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder} --emit-resource-map"
  1271	        read:
  1272	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1273	          - "{state_paths.delta_dir}/iter-*.jsonl"
  1274	        write:
  1275	          - "{state_paths.resource_map_output}"
  1276	        guarantee: "Reducer skips cleanly when config.resource_map.emit == false and writes a deterministic resource-map when enabled."
  1277	
  1278	      step_resource_map_coverage_gate:
  1279	        action: "If resource_map_present, compute the Resource Map Coverage Gate synthesis inputs; otherwise preserve the skip note"
  1280	        note: "Cross-check target_files from {spec_folder}/applied/T-*.md against {spec_folder}/resource-map.md. Report touched entries, untouched entries split into expected-by-scope vs gap, and implementation paths absent from the map. Findings raised here use category resource-map-coverage with severity calibrated to the gate outcome."
  1281	
  1282	      step_compile_review_report:
  1283	        action: "Compile review/review-report.md with 9 core sections, a conditional Resource Map Coverage Gate section, and an embedded Planning Packet"
  1284	        instructions: |
  1285	          Create review/review-report.md with the following sections:
  1286	
  1287	          1. **Executive Summary**
  1288	             - Overall verdict: PASS | CONDITIONAL | FAIL
  1289	             - hasAdvisories: true when PASS carries active P2 items
  1290	             - P0/P1/P2 counts (active only)
  1291	             - Review scope summary
  1292	
  1293	          2. **Planning Trigger**
  1294	             - State whether `/speckit:plan` is required
  1295	             - Include a fenced `json` block labeled `Planning Packet`
  1296	             - The packet MUST include: `triggered`, `verdict`, `hasAdvisories`, `activeFindings`, `remediationWorkstreams`, `specSeed`, `planSeed`, `findingClasses`, `affectedSurfacesSeed`, `fixCompletenessRequired`
  1297	             - Build `activeFindings`, `findingClasses`, and `affectedSurfacesSeed` from reducer-owned state plus iteration JSONL `findingDetails`; if a field is missing, mark it `UNKNOWN` instead of inferring it from prose
  1298	
  1299	          3. **Active Finding Registry**
  1300	             - Unified registry for active P0/P1/P2 findings
  1301	             - For each: ID, severity, title, dimension, file:line, evidence, impact, fix recommendation, disposition, findingClass, scopeProof, affectedSurfaceHints
  1302	
  1303	          4. **Remediation Workstreams**
  1304	             - Ordered action groups: P0 first, then P1, with P2 advisories separated
  1305	
  1306	          5. **Spec Seed**
  1307	             - Concrete seed bullets for follow-on spec updates
  1308	
  1309	          6. **Plan Seed**
  1310	             - Concrete starter tasks for `/speckit:plan`
  1311	
  1312	          7. **Traceability Status**
  1313	             - Summarize required core protocols first, then applicable overlay protocols
  1314	             - Include status, evidence, and any unresolved drift per protocol
  1315	
  1316	          If `resource_map_present` is true, insert **## Resource Map Coverage Gate** after `Traceability Status` with exactly three bullets:
  1317	             - `Entries touched`: count of resource-map paths that appear in applied reports
  1318	             - `Entries not touched`: paths in resource-map not in implementation, labeled `expected-by-scope` or `gap`
  1319	             - `Implementation paths absent from resource-map`: `target_files` in applied reports not on the map
  1320	
  1321	          8. **Deferred Items**
  1322	             - Advisory-only or backlog-worthy follow-ups that do not block the current verdict
  1323	
  1324	          9. **Search Ledger**
  1325	             - Always include this section before the appendix.
  1326	             - Render reducer-owned `searchCoverage`, `candidateCoverage`, `searchDebt`, `ruledOutCandidates`, and `cleanSearchProof`.
  1327	             - If all arrays are empty or absent, include: `*No search-depth state captured (legacy v1 record)*`.
  1328	             - When `searchDebt` is non-empty, state `hasSearchDebt: true` and carry the dashboard verdict as CONDITIONAL unless active P0 findings make it FAIL.
  1329	
  1330	          10. **Audit Appendix**
  1331	             - Convergence summary, coverage summary, ruled-out claims, sources reviewed
  1332	             - Cross-reference appendix MUST be split into `Core Protocols` and `Overlay Protocols`
  1333	
  1334	          Preserve machine-owned markers so the reducer-owned registry, dashboard, and strategy anchors can be refreshed on re-run without mutating prior synthesis. (Note: `completed-continue` lineage branch is deferred — see `deep-review/references/protocol/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches`.)
  1335	        output: "{state_paths.review_output}"
  1336	
  1337	      step_convergence_report:
  1338	        action: "Append convergence report to JSONL"
  1339	        append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"synthesis_complete","mode":"review","totalIterations":{iteration_count},"activeP0":{active_p0},"activeP1":{active_p1},"activeP2":{active_p2},"dimensionCoverage":{dimension_coverage},"verdict":"{verdict}","releaseReadinessState":"{release_readiness_state}","stopReason":"{reason}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'

  1342	      step_update_config_status:

  1347	      step_stage_artifact_dir:
  1348	        action: "Stage all skill-owned artifacts under artifact_dir for the operator's next git commit"
  1349	        command: "git add {state_paths.artifact_dir}"
  1350	        note: "Skill-owned contract fix (028): without this stage step, operators who do `git add review-report.md` lose the iteration trail (iterations/, deltas/, state.jsonl, strategy, findings-registry, etc.). Staging the whole artifact_dir keeps the audit trail intact. Operators retain commit authority and can `git restore --staged {path}` to unstage anything before commit. Failure is non-fatal — log warning and continue."
  1351	        on_fail: "Log warning. Do not block synthesis. The artifact_dir may be outside a git repo (e.g., temp test fixture) which is a valid case."

     1	---
     2	description: Autonomous deep-review loop: iterative code audit with convergence detection. Modes :auto, :confirm.
     3	argument-hint: "<target> [:auto|:confirm] [--max-iterations=N] [--convergence=N] [--spec-folder=PATH] [--executor=<type> --count=N --label=X ...] [--executors=<json>] [--concurrency=N] (:auto supports PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS: prompt-body block for non-interactive setup)"
     4	allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, Task, memory_context, memory_search, code_graph_query, code_graph_context
     5	---
     6	
     7	> **Code Graph ownership:** `code_graph_query` and `code_graph_context` stay stable MCP tool IDs; implementation and docs now live under `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/`.
     8	
     9	> **EXECUTION PROTOCOL -- READ FIRST**
    10	>
    11	> This command runs a structured YAML workflow. Do NOT dispatch agents from this document.
    12	> Under `:auto`, setup follows the three-tier resolution contract in §0: resolve confidently, ask only targeted ambiguity questions, then fail fast if required inputs remain unresolved. Under `:confirm`, setup keeps the consolidated interactive question block.
    13	>
    14	> **YOUR FIRST ACTION:**
    15	> 1. Run the unified setup phase in this Markdown entrypoint and resolve:
    16	>    - `review_target`
    17	>    - `review_target_type`
    18	>    - `review_dimensions`
    19	>    - `spec_folder`
    20	>    - `execution_mode`
    21	>    - `maxIterations`
    22	>    - `convergenceThreshold`
    23	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    24	>    - Auto: `deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`
    25	>    - Confirm: `deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml`
    26	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    27	>
    28	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    29	
    30	## CONSTRAINTS
    31	
    32	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    33	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    34	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    35	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    36	  - `review_target`, `review_target_type`, `review_dimensions`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    37	
    38	> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:start-review-loop:auto`, `/deep:start-review-loop:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, and quick references synchronized to this entrypoint.
    39	
    40	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    41	
    42	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    43	
    44	---
    45	
    46	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    47	
    48	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    49	
    50	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    51	
    52	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    53	
    54	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    55	
    56	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    57	
    58	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` (shape: `reviewTarget`, `reviewTargetType`, `reviewDimensions`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    59	
    60	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `review_target_type`, `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: if `review_target_type` is ambiguous, ask only for `review_target_type` first — the answer may make `spec_folder` self-evident on the next Tier 1 pass. Missing `review_target` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    61	
    62	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-review-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    63	
    64	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the Consolidated Setup Prompt section below.
    65	
    66	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    67	
    68	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults.
    69	
    70	```yaml
    71	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    72	  review_target: <spec-folder>
    73	  review_target_type: spec-folder  # one of: spec-folder | skill | agent | track | files
    74	  review_dimensions: all  # or comma-separated subset: correctness, security, traceability, maintainability
    75	  spec_folder: existing  # one of: existing | new | update-related | phase-folder, or an explicit specs/.opencode/specs path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.10
    79	  executor: native  # one of: native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional, executor-specific
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # optional
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules:
    88	
    89	- Any unspecified field falls back to its documented default.
    90	- Marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags because the caller explicitly bound setup in the prompt body.
    91	- Unknown fields are warnings, not errors.
    92	- Malformed lines, including missing `:`, emit a parse error naming the offending line. Known fields parsed before the error may still be used, and unresolved fields continue to Tier 2 or Tier 3.
    93	- Empty strings count as unresolved for required fields.
    94	- Compact legacy answer strings are only for the consolidated `:confirm` prompt. They are not a `:auto` marker format.
    95	
    96	### Default Resolution Table
    97	
    98	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    99	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
   100	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
   101	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
   102	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
   103	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
   104	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
   105	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
   106	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
   107	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
   108	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   109	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   110	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   111	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   112	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   113	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117	
   118	### Consolidated Setup Prompt for `:confirm` and No-Suffix Mode
   119	
   120	Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
   121	
   122	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   123	
   124	```
   125	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   126	
   127	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   128	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   129	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   130	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
   131	
   132	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
   133	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   134	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   135	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   136	
   137	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   138	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   139	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   140	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   141	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   143	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   144	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   145	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   146	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   149	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
   150	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   151	   |
   152	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   154	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   158	
   159	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   160	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   161	   - The generated `deep-review-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   162	
   163	   Parsing to config mapping:
   164	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   165	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   166	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   167	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   168	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   169	
   170	   Validation hook:
   171	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   172	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   173	
   174	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   175	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   176	
   177	5. Search for prior work (background):
   178	   - memory_context({ input: review_target OR "deep-review", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   179	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]

    19	>    - `convergenceThreshold`
    20	> 2. Load the corresponding YAML file from `assets/` only after all setup values are resolved:
    21	>    - Auto: `deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`
    22	>    - Confirm: `deep_start-research-loop_confirm.yaml`
    23	> 3. Execute the YAML workflow step by step using those resolved values
    24	>
    25	> All content below is reference context for the YAML workflow. Do not treat reference sections as direct instructions to execute.
    26	
    27	## CONSTRAINTS
    28	
    29	- **DO NOT** dispatch any agent from this document
    30	- **ALL** agent dispatching is handled by the YAML workflow steps
    31	- **MARKDOWN OWNS SETUP**: resolve setup inputs here first, then hand off to YAML
    32	- **YAML START CONDITION**: do not load YAML until ALL required inputs are bound:
    33	  - `research_topic`, `spec_folder`, `execution_mode`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`
    34	
    35	> **Canonical mode syntax:** use attached command suffixes (`/deep:start-research-loop:auto`, `/deep:start-research-loop:confirm`) and keep AGENTS, skills, and quick references synchronized to this entrypoint.
    36	
    37	> **Note:** Late-INIT `spec.md` detection and bounded `spec.md` mutations follow
    38	> `.opencode/skills/deep-research/references/protocol/spec_check_protocol.md`.
    39	> Acquire `{spec_folder}/research/.deep-research.lock` before `folder_state` classification and keep `research/research.md` as the source of truth for any generated findings sync.
    40	> Targeted strict validation after deep-research spec mutations uses the Node validation orchestrator; the current strict path is designed for fast packet checks and was measured at about 108ms for a fresh Level 3 packet on the local harness.
    41	
    42	# SINGLE CONSOLIDATED SETUP PROMPT
    43	
    44	This workflow gathers all setup inputs in one prompt. Confirm mode still includes multiple approval gates after setup; only the setup round-trip is consolidated here.
    45	
    46	---
    47	
    48	## 0. UNIFIED SETUP PHASE
    49	
    50	**FIRST MESSAGE PROTOCOL**: For `:confirm` or no suffix, the consolidated setup prompt MUST be your FIRST response. No implementation or file-modifying tool calls before asking. Lightweight read-only discovery to suggest a spec folder or load prior context is allowed, then ask ALL questions immediately and wait.
    51	
    52	For `:auto`, do not emit the consolidated prompt by default. Resolve setup with the three-tier branch below, then load the auto YAML only after all required values are bound.
    53	
    54	### `:auto` Setup Resolution
    55	
    56	Setup contract: see `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/workflows/auto_mode_contract.md`.
    57	
    58	Under `execution_mode = AUTONOMOUS` (from the `:auto` suffix), follow the three-tier flow:
    59	
    60	1. **Tier 1 — Resolve confidently** (contract §1): parse `$ARGUMENTS` flags + `PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:` block (§2) + the Default Resolution Table below (§3). When every required field is resolved, persist to `{artifact_dir}/deep-research-config.json` (shape: `researchTopic`, `specFolder`, `maxIterations`, `convergenceThreshold`, `executionMode: "auto"`, `resource_map.emit`, `config.executor.*`), bind runtime YAML placeholders, set `STATUS: PASSED`, load `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-research-loop_auto.yaml`. End §0.
    61	
    62	2. **Tier 2 — Targeted ask** (contract §1): when 1-2 required fields are genuinely ambiguous AND no default exists, emit ONE narrow question per ambiguous field. Command-specific Tier-2-eligible fields (per the Default Resolution Table below): `spec_folder`. **Ordering rule**: none needed. Missing `research_topic` is absence, not ambiguity — go to Tier 3.
    63	
    64	3. **Tier 3 — Fail fast** (contract §4): emit the named-missing-inputs error format with `/deep:start-research-loop:auto` as the command name. Exit non-zero. Do not load YAML.
    65	
    66	`:confirm` path stays unchanged — see the consolidated setup prompt section below.
    67	
    68	### PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS Schema (for `:auto` non-interactive dispatch)
    69	
    70	The dispatched prompt body may contain one structured marker block. Parse it before applying defaults. Grammar: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2.
    71	
    72	```yaml
    73	PRE-BOUND SETUP ANSWERS:
    74	  research_topic: WebSocket reconnection strategies  # string
    75	  spec_folder: <spec-folder>  # existing | new | update-related | phase-folder | explicit path
    76	  execution_mode: AUTONOMOUS  # from :auto suffix
    77	  maxIterations: 10  # positive integer
    78	  convergenceThreshold: 0.05  # decimal 0..1
    79	  executor: native  # native | cli-codex | cli-gemini | cli-claude-code | cli-opencode | cli-devin
    80	  executor_model: ""  # optional executor-specific model id
    81	  executor_reasoning: ""  # optional reasoning effort
    82	  executor_service_tier: ""  # optional service tier
    83	  executor_timeout: 900  # optional positive integer seconds
    84	  resource_map_emit: true  # boolean
    85	```
    86	
    87	Rules: see `auto_mode_contract.md` §2 (unspecified fields fall back to default; marker fields take precedence over `$ARGUMENTS` flags; unknown fields warn; malformed lines parse-error).
    88	
    89	### Default Resolution Table
    90	
    91	| Field | Required | Resolves Via | Default | Tier-2 Candidate |
    92	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
    93	| `research_topic` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` positional topic, or marker `research_topic` | none | N |
    94	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or targeted choice among suggested existing/new/update-related/phase folder | none | Y, when topic is present but folder choice is ambiguous |
    95	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
    96	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `10` | N |
    97	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.05` | N |
    98	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
    99	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   100	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   101	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   102	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   103	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   104	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each `--executor` group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   105	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   106	
   107	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-research` agent dispatches with isolated dirs. CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses concurrently in the capped pool.
   108	
   109	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   110	
   111	```
   112	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   113	
   114	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   115	   |-- ":auto"         -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   116	   |-- ":confirm"      -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   117	   +-- No suffix       -> execution_mode = "ASK"
   118	
   119	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for topic:
   120	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   121	   |     -> research_topic = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   122	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   123	
   124	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   125	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   126	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   127	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   128	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   129	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   130	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   131	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   132	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   133	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   134	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   135	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   136	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch for complex configs
   137	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   138	   |
   139	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   140	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   141	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   142	   |
   143	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=10, convergenceThreshold=0.05, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   144	
   145	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   146	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults
   147	   - The generated `deep-research-config.json` stores executor settings under `config.executor.*`
   148	
   149	   Parsing to config mapping:
   150	   - `--executor` -> `config.executor.type`
   151	   - `--model` -> `config.executor.model`
   152	   - `--reasoning-effort` -> `config.executor.reasoningEffort`
   153	   - `--service-tier` -> `config.executor.serviceTier`
   154	   - `--executor-timeout` -> `config.executor.timeoutSeconds`
   155	
   156	   Validation hook:
   157	   - `parseExecutorConfig` from `.opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` runs at config-write time
   158	   - Invalid combinations fail fast with clear errors, including `cli-codex` without `--model` and reserved-but-unwired executor kinds
   159	
   160	4. Search for related spec folders across alias roots:
   161	   $ find specs .opencode/specs -mindepth 2 -maxdepth 2 -type d 2>/dev/null | sort | tail -10
   162	
   163	5. Search for prior work (background):
   164	   - memory_context({ input: research_topic OR "deep-research", mode: "focused", includeContent: true })
   165	   - Store: prior_work_found = [yes/no]
   166	
   167	6. ASK with SINGLE prompt (include only applicable questions):
   168	   - Include Q-Exec only when `--executor` is NOT present and the topic text does NOT already mention executor hints such as `cli-codex`, `codex`, or `gpt-5.4`
   169	   - If Q-Exec is omitted and no executor is otherwise resolved, default to `native`
   170	
   171	   Q0. Research Topic (if not in command): What topic to research deeply?
   172	
   173	   Q1. Spec Folder (required):
   174	     A) Use existing [suggest if found]
   175	     B) Create new under `specs/[track]/[###]-[slug]/` (accept `.opencode/specs/` alias roots when already in use)
   176	     C) Update related [if match found]
   177	     D) Phase folder (e.g., `specs/NN-track/NNN-name/001-phase/` or matching `.opencode/specs/` alias)
   178	
   179	   Q2. Execution Mode (if no suffix):
   180	     A) Autonomous -- all iterations without approval

codex
The stronger issue is not just “synthesis might be thin.” A failed CLI lineage currently resolves as a successful pool item unless the JS worker throws, which means the downstream merge can see zero registries and still produce a clean review verdict. I’m re-reading the exact lines before classifying it.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
  1173	      step_fanout_merge:
  1174	        description: "Merge N lineage registries with strongest-restriction; bind p0/p1/p2 counts for step_derive_verdict; skip when single-executor"
  1175	        skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
  1176	        command: |
  1177	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
  1178	            --loop-type review \
  1179	            --artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
  1180	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
  1181	        wait_for_completion: true
  1182	        bind_from_output:
  1183	          p0_count: "active_p0"
  1184	          p1_count: "active_p1"
  1185	          p2_count: "active_p2"
  1186	
  1187	      step_hydrate_summary_metrics:
  1188	        action: "Hydrate summary metrics before synthesis"
  1189	        read:
  1190	          - "{state_paths.config}"
  1191	          - "{state_paths.state_log}"
  1192	          - "{state_paths.findings_registry}"
  1193	          - "{state_paths.strategy}"
  1226	      step_read_all_iterations:
  1227	        action: "Read all iteration files and strategy"
  1228	        read_pattern: "{state_paths.iteration_dir}/iteration-*.md"
  1229	        read_also: "{state_paths.strategy}"

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs | sed -n '304,348p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    27	Run each lineage as the existing command (verbatim loop) into its own isolated sub-packet.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `fanout-pool.cjs` spawn path: existing deep-loop command, single synthesized `config.executor` per lineage, `--max-iterations {lineage.iterations ?? default}`, env `SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID={label}`, capture stdout to `{sub-packet}/logs/iter-NNN.out`.
    31	- `--artifact-dir-override` branch in `step_resolve_artifact_root` for all 4 YAMLs: `deep_start-{research,review}-loop_{auto,confirm}.yaml` (override → use it; else `resolveArtifactRoot`). Sub-packets at `{artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/`.
    32	- Recursion-guard check: distinct kinds safe; same-kind replicas verify loop-lock/ancestry (`SPECKIT_<KIND>_STATE_DIR`).
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- Integration: pool spawns a stub command twice into two `lineages/{label}/` trees; distinct trees + distinct sessionIds + no lock contention.

 succeeded in 0ms:
   304	  const { results, summary } = await runCappedPool({
   305	    items: cliLineages,
   306	    concurrency: fanoutConfig.concurrency,
   307	    onEvent: (event) => appendStatusLedger(ledgerPath, event),
   308	    worker: async (lineage) => {
   309	      const lineageDir = path.join(lineagesDir, lineage.label);
   310	      const stateDir = path.join(lineageDir, '.executor-state');
   311	      fs.mkdirSync(lineageDir, { recursive: true });
   312	      fs.mkdirSync(stateDir, { recursive: true });
   313	
   314	      const sessionId = `fanout-${lineage.label}-${runId}`;
   315	      const prompt = buildLoopPrompt(loopType, specFolder, lineageDir, sessionId, lineage);
   316	
   317	      const resolvedSandbox = resolveCodexSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   318	      const resolvedPermission = resolveClaudePermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   319	      const resolvedGeminiSandbox = resolveGeminiSandboxMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   320	      const resolvedDevinPermission = resolveDevinPermissionMode(lineage.sandboxMode);
   321	
   322	      const effectiveSandbox = lineage.kind === 'cli-gemini' ? resolvedGeminiSandbox : resolvedSandbox;
   323	      const effectivePermission = lineage.kind === 'cli-devin' ? resolvedDevinPermission : resolvedPermission;
   324	
   325	      const { command, args: cmdArgs, input } = buildLineageCommand(
   326	        lineage,
   327	        prompt,
   328	        effectiveSandbox,
   329	        effectivePermission,
   330	      );
   331	
   332	      // Isolate per-kind state dirs so same-kind replicas do not collide on lockfiles.
   333	      const stateEnvKey = SPECKIT_STATE_ENV_BY_KIND[lineage.kind];
   334	      const extraEnv = {
   335	        SPECKIT_FANOUT_LINEAGE_ID: lineage.label,
   336	        ...(stateEnvKey ? { [stateEnvKey]: stateDir } : {}),
   337	      };
   338	
   339	      const timeoutMs = computeLineageTimeoutMs(lineage);
   340	
   341	      const result = spawnSync(command, cmdArgs, {
   342	        cwd: process.cwd(),
   343	        encoding: 'utf8',
   344	        timeout: timeoutMs,
   345	        env: { ...process.env, ...extraEnv },
   346	        maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024,
   347	        ...(typeof input === 'string' ? { input } : {}),
   348	      });

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/004-salvage-coverage-graph/spec.md | sed -n '27,35p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    27	Make every iteration yield a usable artifact even when an executor doesn't write its file (weak CLIs / sandboxed writes), and ensure parallel lineages don't collide on the shared coverage SQLite.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `fanout-pool.cjs` salvage step: after each lineage sub-loop, sweep with `post-dispatch-validate.ts#validateIterationOutputs`; on `iteration_file_missing/empty`, parse `{sub-packet}/logs/iter-NNN.out` (opencode `--format json` text parts, else raw) → write missing `iterations/iteration-NNN.md` + append `salvaged_from_stdout` event; re-validate; still-missing ⇒ failed marker (loop's stuck-recovery handles persistence).
    31	- Coverage-graph: confirm each lineage's own `session_id` scopes `convergence.cjs` + coverage writes (PK/UNIQUE already include session_id) — no schema change; rely on WAL + existing writer lock.
    32	
    33	## Success
    34	- Unit: missing-md+stdout ⇒ salvaged; still-missing ⇒ failed marker.
    35	- Integration: two lineages, distinct sessionIds, shared `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`, explicit no-collision assertion.

.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:537:               h) candidateCoverageGate: skip entirely when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 on the latest iteration. Otherwise pass when the current iteration's `reviewDepthApplicability.scopeClass` is `trivial` with `enforcement: "skip"` AND `reviewDepthApplicability.evidenceRefs` is non-empty, OR when the reducer registry's `searchDebt` is empty AND `candidateCoverage.covered` is a superset of `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` for the lineage. Otherwise pass = false; record `searchDebt` items as blocked-stop evidence; blocker name = "candidateCoverageGate"; recovery_strategy = "Resolve deferred/blocked obligations in next iteration or downgrade scope explicitly to trivial with cited evidence."
.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_confirm.yaml:538:               i) graphlessFallbackGate: skip when `reviewDepthSchemaVersion` is not 2 OR the reducer registry's last-iteration `searchCoverage.graphCoverageMode` is `graph`. Applies when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` or `unavailable_blocked`. Pass when graphCoverageMode is `graphless_fallback` AND at least one `searchLedger` row exists for each entry in `searchCoverage.requiredBugClasses` with `searchActions[].method` in {"direct_read","exact_grep","semantic_search","code_graph_status_check","producer_consumer_trace","negative_test_inspection"} AND non-empty `evidenceRefs`. Fail automatically when graphCoverageMode is `unavailable_blocked`; record the explicit unavailability reason. Otherwise pass = false; blocker name = "graphlessFallbackGate"; recovery_strategy = "Add cited fallback ledger rows (direct read, exact search, producer/consumer trace, negative-test inspection) or set graphCoverageMode to 'graph' once graph data is available."

codex
## Focus
integration synthesis

## Findings
P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:360, Non-zero lineage subprocess exits are returned as normal worker output, so `fanout-pool.cjs:86` marks the item `fulfilled` and `fanout-pool.cjs:209` counts it as succeeded. Downstream, `fanout-merge.cjs:305` skips missing registries and `fanout-merge.cjs:197` can produce PASS from an empty review merge. Fix: reject/mark failed on non-zero child exit, missing registry, or failed salvage, and make merge fail when any requested lineage lacks a valid registry.

P1, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:139, Fan-out jumps directly to synthesis and skips `phase_init`/`phase_main_loop`, but synthesis still reads base `state_log`/`strategy` at `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1191` and base iterations at `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1228`; `fanout-merge.cjs:316` only writes the merged registry. Successful lineage artifacts remain under `lineages/{label}`, so the existing synthesis path is not actually fan-out-aware. Fix: have merge materialize base rollup state/strategy/iteration inputs, or add a dedicated fan-out synthesis path that reads lineage sub-packets directly.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, CLI fan-out uses `spawnSync` inside the async pool worker, so subprocess execution blocks the Node event loop and the configured concurrency does not create true parallel lineage execution. This contradicts the pool/lineage contract in `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:31` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:35`. Fix: switch the lineage worker to async `spawn`, resolve on `close`, and preserve timeout/kill handling.

## Verdict
FAIL: the highest residual risk is a false successful fan-out when lineage subprocesses fail or produce no registry.

Review verdict: FAIL

===FINDINGS_JSON===

===END_FINDINGS===

## Focus
integration synthesis

## Findings
P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:360, Non-zero lineage subprocess exits are returned as normal worker output, so `fanout-pool.cjs:86` marks the item `fulfilled` and `fanout-pool.cjs:209` counts it as succeeded. Downstream, `fanout-merge.cjs:305` skips missing registries and `fanout-merge.cjs:197` can produce PASS from an empty review merge. Fix: reject/mark failed on non-zero child exit, missing registry, or failed salvage, and make merge fail when any requested lineage lacks a valid registry.

P1, .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:139, Fan-out jumps directly to synthesis and skips `phase_init`/`phase_main_loop`, but synthesis still reads base `state_log`/`strategy` at `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1191` and base iterations at `.opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:1228`; `fanout-merge.cjs:316` only writes the merged registry. Successful lineage artifacts remain under `lineages/{label}`, so the existing synthesis path is not actually fan-out-aware. Fix: have merge materialize base rollup state/strategy/iteration inputs, or add a dedicated fan-out synthesis path that reads lineage sub-packets directly.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs:341, CLI fan-out uses `spawnSync` inside the async pool worker, so subprocess execution blocks the Node event loop and the configured concurrency does not create true parallel lineage execution. This contradicts the pool/lineage contract in `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/002-capped-pool-status-ledger/spec.md:31` and `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/003-per-lineage-spawn-isolation/spec.md:35`. Fix: switch the lineage worker to async `spawn`, resolve on `close`, and preserve timeout/kill handling.

## Verdict
FAIL: the highest residual risk is a false successful fan-out when lineage subprocesses fail or produce no registry.

Review verdict: FAIL
